---
title: "Hello, writing"
date: "2026-05-09"
description: "First test post — verifying that the Writing feed actually picks up new entries and renders them right."
categories: [meta]
---

This is the first post on the Writing page. If you can read it on the live site, the listing pipeline is working: a new `.qmd` file dropped into `posts/`, pushed, picked up by the Quarto build, and rendered on `/writing/` with title, date, and description.

## Things this post is checking

A handful of formatting features get exercised below so we can see whether the theme renders them cleanly.

### Inline emphasis

Italics with *single asterisks*. Bold with **double asterisks**. A link to [the Quarto docs](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-listings.html) so we can see how external links style.

### A callout

::: {.callout-note}
This is a Quarto callout. If it shows up as a bordered box with a "Note" label, callouts work.
:::

### A code block

```r
# A quick R block to verify syntax highlighting
df <- data.frame(x = 1:5, y = c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"))
summary(df)
```

### A blockquote

> Some debts are paid back forward, in installments, to strangers.

### A small list

When lists are unavoidable:

- First item, with a [link](https://example.com) for kicks
- Second item
- Third item

---

If everything above renders cleanly, the writing pipeline is good and we can start moving real essays in.
